cards.back: A type of experimental design where each treatment is applied to every block, and every block has the same probability of being assigned to any treatment.
Dividing the experimental units into groups (blocks) to reduce the effect of confounding variables and increase the precision of the analysis.
Assigning treatments randomly to experimental units, ensuring that each unit has an equal probability of being assigned to any treatment.
Performing multiple experiments with the same treatment, under the same conditions, to increase the precision and reliability of the results.
A measure of the number of independent pieces of information in a statistical analysis, used in calculating the F-statistic in ANOVA.
